fields.description:
## Symptom
Under `verify.enforce_plan_material_pins=true`, the plan-review CLOSE gate rejects a valid,
unchanged **planning-phase** attestation with `incompatible-phase` (`planning -> execution`),
even though the plan material never drifted. Closing then requires a fresh execution-phase
review that cannot be minted after code drift (the drift-refresh path re-signs the prior
planning manifest), so the ticket cannot be closed.

## Root cause — an undocumented override that contradicts the design
The phase mechanism (ticket 5967, "Add planning and execution phases") and the close gate
(ticket 2bbd, "Require a separately attested current plan review at close") deliberately make a
planning attestation COMPATIBLE with an execution-phase ticket at close. `docs/plan-review-gate.md`
§"phase_status compatibility" documents exactly this: `execution | planning or legacy planning |
compatible`. 2bbd's stated purpose is to catch a plan that CHANGED during execution — via
own-material + pin drift — "without invalidating every implementation because implementation
files changed." It never required a fresh execution-phase review.

`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py::compute_validity` contains a later override (added by
ticket 145e, "enable ... enforcement for rebar", gated on `enforced`) that forces
`phase_status = "incompatible"` when `profile is CLOSE and signed_phase == "planning"`. This
directly contradicts the documented table and 2bbd/5967's design, and is documented nowhere.
The one concern cited in 145e's trail ("an in-progress self-edit must invalidate close until a
fresh execution review") is already covered by own-material-drift invalidation, so the override
is broader than any real requirement.

## Fix
Remove the `enforced`-gated CLOSE override (and the paired CLOSE-only `current_phase` promotion
to "execution") so the CLOSE profile uses the same documented `review_phase_status` table as
DEFAULT/DRIFT_REFRESH. A planning attestation certifies close iff the plan material/pins have not
drifted; a drifted plan still invalidates and forces a re-review. The implementation is validated
independently by the completion-verifier close gate. (Secondary, out of scope here: the drift-refresh
path is reached even under `--force`, contradicting the `__init__.py` comment that `force` bypasses
it; noted for a follow-up but not required once the gate accepts either phase.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Under `enforce_plan_material_pins=true`, `close_plan_review_gate_check` returns
      `ok=true, verdict="certified"` for an unchanged planning-phase attestation.
- [ ] A plan/pin change during execution still invalidates at close (material/pin drift), forcing
      a re-review — unchanged.
- [ ] The `planning`-current + `execution`-signed row stays `incompatible` (base table unchanged).
- [ ] `docs/plan-review-gate.md` states the CLOSE profile uses the same compatibility table.
- [ ] Tests that encoded the undocumented override are inverted to the documented behavior.

## File impact
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py
- tests/unit/test_plan_review_phase_validity.py
- tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_policy_dogfood.py
- docs/plan-review-gate.md
fields.title: plan-review CLOSE gate rejects a valid unchanged planning-phase attestation (undocumented override contradicts 5967/2bbd design + docs)
